summary: I reviewed the Define-phase artifacts (`requirements.md` R1–R50, `discovery_notes.md`, `open_questions.md`) against `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`. Since my iteration-1 pass, the only material change is the newly-added human-in-the-loop modal section (R36–R50), so I focused there: R37 (modal overlay), R48 (focus management/keyboard/AT), and R49 (shared styling/components) all track the UI guide's modal, accessibility, and single-source-of-truth rules, and `open_questions.md` Q11–Q14 keep the change UI-only, routing answers through the existing `Phases::AnswerQuestions` service so business logic stays in a reusable PORO with a thin controller — consistent with the backend guide. The three panels remain aligned as before (aggregate reads to `app/queries/`, thin `HomeController#index`, broadcasts from services after commit, reuse of `StatusHelper#status_badge` and the prescribed card/badge/type-scale/empty-state conventions, status never by color alone per R12/R35). I found no current guide deviation — every requirement is implementable compliantly — so the **verdict is pass** with empty findings, and I recorded one non-blocking watch-item in the summary for Plan/Build: R40/R42's "default text shown in place until the user types" reads toward a placeholder-as-value pattern that would risk the a11y contrast/semantic-field rules, and Plan should instead implement the default as a real pre-filled editable value (which satisfies R40–R43 accessibly).
